machine.name: Case Worker Capacity Monitor
machine.description: Monitors active caseload ratios, response time performance, worker availability, and case complexity distribution for community service case workers and teams. Case worker capacity is the primary throughput constraint in community services — when workers are overloaded, intake delays, missed follow-ups, and compliance failures cascade downstream. Output at [968:972] feeds the Service Delivery Integrity Monitor.

metadata.domain: Community Services — Case Management / Worker Capacity
metadata.author: Reality Engine
metadata.populationFocus: Community services organizations with case management functions; public agency benefit offices; non-profit care coordinators; social services departments

sensorNormalization.caseload_ratio_norm: 0.0=>150% of recommended caseload per worker (e.g. 75+ cases where 50 is standard), 0.5=100–120% of recommended caseload (manageable but over), 1.0=<90% of recommended caseload (adequate capacity)
sensorNormalization.response_time_norm: 0.0=average initial contact response >10 business days (regulatory threshold exceeded), 0.5=response within 3–5 business days (borderline), 1.0=response within 1–2 business days (meeting or exceeding standard)
sensorNormalization.worker_availability_norm: 0.0=>25% of case worker positions vacant or on leave; no coverage plan, 0.5=10–25% positions vacant; coverage partial, 1.0=<10% positions vacant; full coverage maintained
sensorNormalization.complexity_index_norm: 0.0=majority of caseload is high-complexity (trauma, substance use, serious mental illness, homelessness), 0.5=mixed complexity; high-complexity cases at or above 40% of caseload, 1.0=balanced complexity distribution; high-complexity cases below 30%

governance.notes: Auto-backfilled by scripts/backfill-governance.mjs from primaryDomain="community-services".  Review and tune the routing as the team owning CaseWorkerCapacityMonitor matures.

rules.description: CAPACITY_CRISIS: case worker capacity failure threatening service delivery and regulatory compliance at [968:972]
OVERLOADED: sustained case worker overload degrading service quality at [968:972]
CAPACITY_ADEQUATE: case worker capacity supports quality service delivery at [968:972]

sequences.name: Capacity Collapse: ADEQUATE+ → STRAINED → OVERLOADED_STATE → CAPACITY_CRISIS

metadata.description: Tracks case worker capacity collapse from an adequate baseline through strain and overload to full crisis. Crisis conditions arise from staff attrition, funding cuts that prevent backfill, or sudden intake surges (disaster events, policy changes creating new eligible populations). Requires ADEQUATE+ baseline before crisis escalation arms.

metadata.name: ADEQUATE
metadata.description: ADEQUATE+ — caseload within recommended range; response times meeting standards; staffing adequate; complexity manageable. isInitial A*A = A+.

metadata.name: STRAINED
metadata.description: STRAINED — caseload above recommended; response times slipping; partial vacancies; complexity concentration rising.

metadata.name: OVERLOADED_STATE
metadata.description: OVERLOADED_STATE — caseload severely over standard; response times violating regulatory thresholds; significant vacancies; high-complexity cases dominating. Fires CAPACITY_CRISIS.
CAPACITY_CRISIS: case worker capacity failure threatening service delivery and regulatory compliance at [968:972]
metadata.action: Emergency staffing plan; case redistribution; intake hold for non-critical cases; supervisor escalation; regulatory notification if thresholds exceeded

metadata.description: Fires OVERLOADED when caseload consistently exceeds recommended levels without reaching full crisis — the chronic overload state that characterizes many under-resourced community services organizations. Sustained overload without crisis-level vacancy enables service delivery to continue but at degraded quality and compliance risk.

metadata.name: SUSTAINED_OVERLOAD
metadata.description: SUSTAINED_OVERLOAD+ — caseload 120–140% of recommended; response times borderline; staffing near-full but case complexity high. isInitial A*A = A+: fires OVERLOADED each cycle.
metadata.scenario: Team of 8 workers carrying caseloads averaging 65 cases each (standard: 50); no vacancies but all at capacity ceiling

metadata.description: OVERLOADED: sustained case worker overload degrading service quality at [968:972]
metadata.action: Hiring request escalation; case complexity review; caseload rebalancing; supervisor check-in schedule increase; burnout monitoring

metadata.description: Fires CAPACITY_ADEQUATE when caseloads are within recommended limits, response times are meeting standards, staffing is near-full, and case complexity is balanced. Downstream service delivery integrity monitor uses this as its baseline stable-delivery indicator.

metadata.name: STAFFED
metadata.description: STAFFED+ — caseloads within recommended range; response times meeting 2-business-day standard; <10% vacancy; complexity balanced. Fires CAPACITY_ADEQUATE each cycle.
metadata.scenario: Fully staffed team; grant funding covering backfill; new hire in orientation; caseloads averaging 45 per worker

metadata.description: CAPACITY_ADEQUATE: case worker capacity supports quality service delivery at [968:972]
metadata.action: Service delivery integrity monitor registers staffing adequate

inputSequences.name: Staff Attrition Cascade
inputSequences.pattern: 5-step: ADEQUATE→ADEQUATE→STRAINED (two departures)→OVERLOADED (third departure, no backfill)→ADEQUATE (after hiring)
inputSequences.description: Three experienced case workers leave within two months; backfill delayed by hiring freeze; capacity crisis triggers emergency contract worker hire; restored to adequate.
